Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendants' Supplemental Expert Disclosure
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alexandra Hamilton, Esq. (415-673-4800) for Plaintiff/Moving party HEATHER ROGERS AND BRIAN ROGERS.
Robert O'Brien, Esq. (213-436-4888) for Defendant/opposing party through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs Heather Ishimaru Rogers and Brian Rogers' motion to strike defendants KGO Television, Inc. and Tracey Watkowski's supplemental expert disclosure is denied. Defendants' supplemental expert disclosure is proper pursuant to CCP Sec. 2034.280. Defendants have designated two experts, Dr. Kim and Mr. Hernandez, to testify regarding previously undisclosed subject matters identified for the first time in plaintiffs' initial expert disclosures. Whether defendants should have anticipated the subject areas becomes relevant when a motion to augment is required pursuant to CCP Sec. 2034.620 which is not the situation present here. Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR #11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/CPK)

The Motion for Summary Judgment is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT KGO TELEVISION, INC, TRACEY WATKOWSKI's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues is DENIED. Objections to the declarations of Dr. Kim and Dr. Steinberg are SUSTAINED.
